accountable_owners.internal_reviewer_role: internal security lead and legal/vendor-relations approver

disclosure_routing.initial_contact_payload: scope summary, requested evidence categories, redaction policy, authorization request, and non-claim boundary
routing_options: vendor-published security contact verified at send time

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: Requested provenance is not evidence until vendor-authorized artifacts or attestations are received and reviewed.
evidence_request_categories.description: Release artifact identity, version lineage, source/build input binding, dependency locks, CI release path, store package digest where shareable, signing certificate digest, and approval owner.
minimum_review_criteria: artifact family and version are identified without exposing secrets
source revision or internal build label is bound to the release scope
dependency lock inputs and build timestamp are recorded
signing certificate digest or store attestation digest is supplied where shareable
vendor release owner signoff is present
requested_items: release version and platform package identifiers
source revision or internal build label
CI job identity or release pipeline attestation

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: Requested native and biometric policy material is not treated as true until reviewed against redaction and owner signoff criteria.
evidence_request_categories.description: Android/iOS vault, keystore/keychain, biometric prompt, enrollment-change, fallback, hardware-backed, and migration policy evidence for the native signing boundary.
minimum_review_criteria: Android and iOS native vault paths are covered or explicitly scoped out
keystore/keychain accessibility and hardware-backed settings are described
biometric enrollment-change and fallback behavior are tested or attested
migration and backup/restore behavior is described
no seed, key, passcode, or biometric material is included
requested_items: Android Keystore and iOS Keychain accessibility settings
vault migration and key rotation behavior

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: Backend behavior is not accepted as evidence until authorized API contracts, tests, traces, or owner attestations are reviewed.
evidence_request_categories.description: Payload API authorization, one-time resolution, conflict handling, expiry, cancellation, replay rejection, and concurrent race semantics.
minimum_review_criteria: authorization rules for payload creation, fetch, patch, reject, and submit are stated
single-use resolution is atomic or the residual race is explicitly modeled
duplicate PATCH and conflicting submit/reject attempts are covered
expiry is enforced server-side and client-side behavior is distinguished
test or trace summaries cover replay and concurrent race attempts
requested_items: payload API contract or equivalent policy
PATCH conflict and duplicate resolution behavior

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: A signed-build attestation request does not establish byte equivalence, production release approval, or possession of vendor signing material until authorized attestation evidence is reviewed.
evidence_request_categories.description: Owner-approved or cryptographically signed statement binding release artifacts to source/build inputs, native modules, dependency locks, CI environment, signing certificate digest, and store upload.
minimum_review_criteria: attestation signer or approving owner is identified by role
artifact digests and signing certificate digests are present where shareable
build environment and dependency locks are linked to release scope
public Testnet verifier build is not classified as vendor-release equivalent
signing secrets and CI credentials are absent
requested_items: signed release attestation or owner-approved equivalent
build input and dependency lock digest set
store upload or release approval evidence

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: Requested trace summaries are not runtime proof until redacted, reviewed, mapped to model events, and approved by accountable owners.
evidence_request_categories.description: Vendor-reviewed device trace summaries for authentication, vault, review, signing, backend patch, submit, cancellation, expiry, replay, reconnect, and network-change paths.
minimum_review_criteria: fresh test device or declared device baseline is recorded
trace categories map to model events without raw secrets
negative paths for cancellation, expiry, replay, reconnect, and network change are covered
trace reviewer records scope and date
raw seeds, payloads, account addresses, and transaction blobs are absent
requested_items: payload review and backend patch trace summary
submit result and ledger outcome trace summary
cancellation, expiry, replay, reconnect, and network-change trace summaries

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: XRPL/RPC statements are not facts until reviewed and explicitly modeled or accepted as residual trust.
evidence_request_categories.description: Trusted XRPL node/RPC endpoint assumptions, network identity checks, ledger finality interpretation, endpoint compromise impact, fallback behavior, and quorum behavior if any.
minimum_review_criteria: trusted endpoint set or endpoint selection policy is stated
network identity and wrong-network rejection behavior are described
ledger finality and submit-result interpretation are scoped
fallback, quorum, or single-endpoint behavior is explicit
endpoint compromise impact is mapped to model assumptions

evidence_request_categories.claim_boundary: Vulnerability detail, exploit steps, or counterexample payloads must not be sent until the vendor-approved route is verified and authorization is recorded.
evidence_request_categories.description: Vendor-approved security contact, escalation path, embargo expectations, evidence-handling channel, and authorization rules for counterexamples or vulnerability details.
minimum_review_criteria: route is verified at send time
authorized recipient role is recorded
embargo and handling expectations are documented
counterexample detail sharing rules are explicit
internal security lead and legal/vendor-relations approval are recorded
redaction_required: payloads that identify users or accounts

review_criteria.promotion_rule: Only PORTAL-CXTP-153 may promote received vendor-authorized evidence into a manifest or review artifact after redaction, digest binding, owner signoff, and reviewer acceptance.
